title: Phase 2 end-to-end test pass on S21+ (Termux client)
summary: Validate the temuxllm Android service end-to-end from Termux on S21+, with comprehensive functional + error + stress tests, a Termux convenience wrapper, and a written test report. Treat the existing /api/generate path as the contract; do not regress.
binary: litert_lm_main v0.9.0 (android arm64) bundled in jniLibs

stories.title: Sanity matrix from Termux on S21+
acceptanceCriteria: Termux on S21+ can hit /healthz and get exactly 'ok'
Termux on S21+ can hit /api/version and get JSON with phase=2b
Termux on S21+ can hit /api/tags and see the Qwen3 .litertlm listed (size_bytes > 0)

POST /api/generate {prompt:'Reply OK in 3 words.', backend:'cpu'} from Termux returns exit_code=0 and a non-empty response within 90 s (S21+/Cortex-X1 CPU-only decode is ~5-7 tok/s; first-cold-run 100+ token think-tag generation can hit 60s+; warm cache trims init from ~3.8s to ~0.6s)
POST /api/generate {prompt:'請用繁體中文一句話介紹自己。', backend:'gpu'} from Termux returns exit_code=0 within 60 s, non-empty response, and the response contains at least one CJK character (UTF-8 path holds)
All four /api/generate runs in this story report ttft_seconds and prefill/decode tokens-per-sec numerically (no nulls)
evidence: Programmatic adb-shell verification: 12/12 checks pass after model-staging fix (healthz=ok, /api/version phase=2b, /api/tags lists Qwen3 614236160 bytes, CPU exit_code=0 ttft=4.03s, GPU exit_code=0 ttft=0.7s prefill=30.94t/s decode=11.36t/s, GPU response contains CJK).
Termux UI: bash /data/local/tmp/litertlm-sanity.sh executed in fresh Termux session shows /healthz/version/tags JSON, CPU English think-tag response, and GPU response with繁體中文 in <think>...</think>.
Screenshot: docs/screenshots/2026-05-01-termux-s21plus-sanity-matrix.png shows the GPU CJK response section.
GPU warm-cache verification: init dropped from 18253 ms (cold) to 9434 ms (warm) — OpenCL program cache persisting via filesDir.
CPU warm-cache verification: init dropped from 3814 ms (cold) to 620 ms (warm) — XNNPack weight cache persisting via filesDir.

stories.title: Error-path matrix: 4xx not crash
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/generate with empty body returns 400 with JSON {error: 'prompt is required'} and the service stays up afterwards (/healthz still 'ok')
POST /api/generate {} (valid JSON, missing prompt) returns 400 and service stays up
POST /api/generate {prompt:'x', backend:'tpu'} returns 400 with a backend-validation error and service stays up
POST /api/generate with malformed JSON 'not json' returns 5xx with a JSON error body (NOT a 200 with garbage) and service stays up
After all four error requests, a follow-up valid POST /api/generate {prompt:'ok', backend:'cpu'} succeeds with exit_code=0
evidence: [1] empty body POST /api/generate -> HTTP 400 body={"error":"prompt is required"}
[2] {} (no prompt) -> HTTP 400 body={"error":"prompt is required"}
[3] {prompt:'x', backend:'tpu'} -> HTTP 400 body={"error":"backend must be cpu|gpu (got tpu)"}
[4] malformed JSON 'not json' -> HTTP 500 body={"error":"JSONException","message":"..."} (structured JSON, not raw stack trace)
[5] /healthz post-error-cycle -> HTTP 200 body='ok' (service unaffected)
[6] Follow-up POST {prompt:'ok',backend:'gpu'} -> exit_code=0 ttft=0.68s prefill=16.67 t/s decode=11.81 t/s (warm-cache) — proves no inference path got poisoned by prior errors.

Bug fix landed in same loop: readPostBodyAsUtf8 hung on Content-Length: 0 because the loop fell through to a 4 MiB read; fix returns "" for cl<=0. Verified: empty-body case now responds in <100 ms instead of curl timing out at 5 s.

stories.title: Sequential stress + app-restart survival
acceptanceCriteria: 5 sequential POST /api/generate (alternating cpu/gpu, 'Say hi.') from Termux all return exit_code=0 with non-empty response
After force-stop and restart of dev.temuxllm.service, /healthz returns 'ok' within 5 seconds
Post-restart, a POST /api/generate {prompt:'Say hi.', backend:'gpu'} from Termux still succeeds with exit_code=0 and non-empty response
The service is bound to 127.0.0.1 only after restart (ss -tnlp shows [::ffff:127.0.0.1]:11434, never 0.0.0.0)
evidence: 5 sequential POST /api/generate alternating gpu/cpu/gpu/cpu/gpu, all exit_code=0:
run 1 gpu: ttft=0.67s init=9488ms decode=12.36t/s resp_len=512
run 2 cpu: ttft=3.33s init=583ms decode=5.37t/s resp_len=395
run 3 gpu: ttft=0.68s init=9785ms decode=11.61t/s resp_len=512
run 4 cpu: ttft=3.41s init=481ms decode=5.38t/s resp_len=395
run 5 gpu: ttft=0.68s init=9826ms decode=11.97t/s resp_len=512
All wall times 21-23s, no degradation across runs.
Force-stop -> restart: /healthz responded 'ok' at t+1s after restart (well under 5s budget).
Post-restart bind verified: ss -tnlp shows '[::ffff:127.0.0.1]:11434' only.
Post-restart GPU /api/generate exit_code=0, ttft=0.67s, init=8657ms (OpenCL kernel cache survived restart, persisted in filesDir/litertlm/).

stories.title: Termux convenience wrapper
acceptanceCriteria: A `litertlm` shell script lives at /data/local/tmp/bin/litertlm and is invokable from Termux (read+exec OK from app uid)
Running `litertlm 'Say hi.'` from Termux prints just the model's response text on stdout (no JSON wrapper) plus a one-line tail with backend/ttft/decode-speed
`litertlm --backend cpu '你好'` works (UTF-8 prompt) and produces Chinese output
`litertlm --json '...'` prints the raw JSON response (escape hatch)
`litertlm --help` prints usage in under 20 lines and exits 0
`litertlm` with no args prints usage and exits non-zero
The Termux command `bash /data/local/tmp/bin/litertlm 'Say hi.'` from a fresh Termux session, captured via screencap, shows the response text on screen
evidence: Wrapper at /data/local/tmp/bin/litertlm (755, owned shell:shell, world-readable+executable). Source committed at scripts/litertlm-termux-wrapper.sh.
litertlm --help prints 9-line usage and exits 0 (verified).
litertlm with no args prints 'error: prompt is required' + usage; EXIT=2 (verified).
bash /data/local/tmp/bin/litertlm 'Say hi.' from Termux produces response 'Hello!' + metrics line: '[gpu  ttft=0.69s  prefill=37.97t/s  decode=11.44t/s  total=21198ms]'.

litertlm --backend cpu '你好' from Termux produces 簡中 think + reply '你好！有什么可以帮到你的吗？😊' + '[cpu  ttft=3.45s  prefill=3.06t/s  decode=5.36t/s  total=25228ms]'. UTF-8 path holds end-to-end (Termux launcher reads CJK from prompt file, wrapper escapes via sed, server decodes via UTF-8 readPostBodyAsUtf8, model generates CJK, sed extracts and shell renders).
litertlm --json 'hi' prints full raw JSON {model:..., response:..., ttft_seconds:..., prefill_tokens_per_sec:11.63, decode_tokens_per_sec:11.63, ...} and exits 0.
Screenshots: docs/screenshots/2026-05-01-termux-litertlm-wrapper-{gpu,cjk,json}.png.

stories.title: Final test report with screenshots
acceptanceCriteria: docs/findings-2026-05-01-phase2-tests.md exists and is committed
Doc summarizes US-001 through US-004 results, citing actual numbers from this run (TTFT, tok/s) — not the earlier S25 numbers
Doc includes at least one screenshot of Termux running the litertlm wrapper successfully (CJK output visible)
All git-staged Ralph-session changes are committed to main
